title: Grafana Loki Log Query Cardinality Explosion Detection
app: Grafana + Loki, metrics from `loki_discarded_samples_total` mirrored to Splunk optional
spl:
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:log" loki
| search "lines processed" OR "total entries" OR "post-filter" OR "chunk fetch"
| eval heavy=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)high cardinality|large query|max chunks|too many entries"),1,0)
| rex field=_raw "(?i)streams[=:](?<streams>\d+)"
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(heavy) as heavy_hits, median(streams) as med_streams, count as log_lines by _time, org_id
| where heavy_hits > 0 OR med_streams > 5000
| sort -heavy_hits
description: LogQL queries that explode into millions of streams degrade Loki queriers and front-ends, often triggered by unbounded label selectors after deployments. Surfacing heavy-query signatures from Grafana’s Loki client logs protects the shared logging tier.
value: Avoids querier OOM loops and keeps incident investigations fast when Loki backs Explore.
implementation: Prefer shipping Loki `query_stats` JSON if available; map `streams`, `chunks_downloaded`, `queue_time_seconds`. Coach teams to use bounded time ranges and recording rules for high-fanout filters.
visualization: Timechart of heavy_hits, table of offending org_id, histogram of streams.
grandmaExplanation: We look for searches in your log database that accidentally touch too many streams. Those searches can slow down logging for everyone, so we catch the pattern early.
